---
title: "The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{The roxyassert annotation grammar — complete reference}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", eval = FALSE)
```

The authoritative, exhaustive reference for the roxyassert annotation language.
Every construct and combination is written out so the language can be audited
against itself — it must agree with itself everywhere.

## 1. Design invariants

1. **One home per concept.** The element type and its element-level modifiers
   (`in`, `| NA`) and a `vector`'s length live in `<>` (or attach to a bare
   atomic type); the whole-argument modifiers (`?`/`| NULL`, type unions) sit
   outside.
2. **A bare *atomic* type is a vector.** `scalar<...>` is the only way to say
   "length 1". Reference and composite types are not vectors (invariant 7).
3. **`NA` and `NULL` are reserved words, never type names** — this keeps `|`
   unambiguous.
4. **Element-level binds tighter than slot-level.** `in` / `| NA` bind to the
   nearest atom on their left, before any slot-level `|` union or `?`.
5. **The language is recursive.** A composite contains *full annotations* in its
   bullets, to any depth.
6. **Sets are R; intervals are math.** A set is an R expression emitted verbatim
   (`c(...)` or a name); an interval is ISO/Bourbaki bracket notation. A bound or
   set element must evaluate to the **same type** as the atom it constrains
   (a generator-time check, since `rexpr` is opaque).
7. **A type may only carry what its category supports** (section 2). The grammar
   makes nonsensical combinations (an interval on `complex`, a `vector` of
   `function`, `| NA` on `raw`) unrepresentable rather than relying on later
   checks.

## 2. Type categories

Every base type belongs to exactly one category, and the category — refined by
the per-type footnotes — decides which modifiers are legal:

| Category | Types | `in [interval]` | `in c(set)` | `\| NA` | length / `vector<>` / bare | shape |
|---|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|---|
| **Ordered atomic** | `integer` `numeric` `Date` `POSIXct` | ✅ | ✅¹ | ✅ | ✅ | bare / `scalar<>` / `vector<>` |
| **Discrete atomic** | `character` `factor` `complex` `logical` `raw` | ❌ | ✅²ᐟ³ | ✅⁴ | ✅ | bare / `scalar<>` / `vector<>` |
| **Reference** | `function` `R6<Class>` | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | **bare only** (length-1 by nature) |
| **Composite** | `list` `data.table` `data.frame` | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | bare, or refined by **nested bullets** |

¹ Sets compare with `==`/`%in%`: discouraged on `numeric` (floating-point) and on
`Date`/`POSIXct` (double-based, time-zone- and precision-sensitive, no
normalisation) — prefer intervals there. Interval bounds for `Date`/`POSIXct` are
R expressions of the matching class (e.g. `as.Date("2024-01-01")`).
² On `factor` and `character` a set constrains the **realised values**
(`as.character(x) %in% set`), so the two behave identically; it does **not**
assert the factor's declared `levels()`.
³ A set on `logical` is degenerate (its whole domain is `{TRUE, FALSE}`) and is
**forbidden**; sets on `complex`/`raw` are legal but rare.
⁴ `raw` has **no `NA` representation** in R (`is.na()` on a raw vector is always
`FALSE`), so `| NA` is **forbidden** on `raw`.

Consequences the grammar enforces directly: no interval on
`complex`/`character`/`raw`/`factor`/`logical`; no `in`/`| NA`/length on
`function`/`R6`/`list`/`data.table`/`data.frame`; `function`/`R6` are bare length-1
references (never `scalar<>`/`vector<>`); composites are refined only by nested
bullets. The remaining per-type rules (¹–⁴) are generator-time checks.

## 3. Formal grammar (EBNF)

```
annotation     ::= "(" slot ")" bullets?
                   (* static check: bullets present only when slot has exactly
                      one non-NULL alternative and it is a composite type *)

slot           ::= type ( "|" type )*  ( ( "|" "NULL" ) | "?" )?
                   (* a union of >=1 types; the whole argument may be NULL,
                      written EITHER as a "| NULL" alternative OR a trailing "?",
                      never both *)

type           ::= atomic | reference | composite

atomic         ::= atom
                 | "scalar" "<" atom ">"
                 | "vector" "<" atom ( "," length )? ">"
                   (* a bare atom is a vector of any length *)

atom           ::= ordered ( "in" ( interval | set ) )?  ( "|" "NA" )?
                 | nominal ( "in" set )?                  ( "|" "NA" )?
                 | "logical"                              ( "|" "NA" )?
                 | "raw"    ( "in" set )?

ordered        ::= "integer" | "numeric" | "Date" | "POSIXct"
nominal        ::= "character" | "factor" | "complex"

reference      ::= "function" | "R6" "<" ident ">"
composite      ::= "list" | "data.table" | "data.frame"

interval       ::= low bound "," bound high
low            ::= "["    (* closed *) | "]"   (* open *)
high           ::= "]"    (* closed *) | "["   (* open *)
bound          ::= signed_number | "Inf" | "-Inf" | rexpr
                   (* signed_number/Inf for integer & numeric; an R expr of the
                      matching class for Date/POSIXct, e.g. as.Date("2024-01-01") *)
set            ::= rexpr  (* c(...) or a bare identifier name; emitted verbatim *)

length         ::= int          (* exactly n *)
                 | int ".." int (* inclusive range *)
                 | int ".."     (* at least n *)

bullets        ::= bullet+      (* siblings at one indentation depth *)
bullet         ::= "- " name " (" slot ")" ":"? description bullets?
                   (* the deeper-indented bullets follow the same static check
                      as `annotation`: one non-NULL composite alternative *)
name           ::= ident | "**" ident "**"   (* bold optional, no semantic effect *)

(* lexical terminals *)
int            ::= digit+
signed_number  ::= "-"? digit+ ( "." digit+ )?
ident          ::= letter ( letter | digit | "." | "_" )*
rexpr          ::= (* any valid R expression; opaque, emitted verbatim *)
description    ::= (* free text to end of the bullet line *)
```

Notes that make parsing deterministic:

- **`in (interval | set)` and `| NA` are consumed first.** Inside a `vector<...>`
  body the element `atom` — including its `in (interval|set)` and `| NA` — is
  fully consumed before the length is sought. The comma separating the atom from
  the `length` is therefore the **first top-level comma remaining after the atom
  is consumed**, never a comma inside an interval or a set.
- **An interval is one lexical unit.** `low bound , bound high` contains exactly
  one comma, between its two bounds, and is terminated by its closing bracket.
  Its inner comma is part of the interval and invisible to the atom/length split.
  The interval delimiters `[ ] ] [` are **not** counted by the `< >` / `( )`
  generic-depth tracker (so an open interval `]0, Inf[` never unbalances it).
- **`rexpr` regions are fully R-lexed.** A set body (`c(...)` or a name) and a
  `Date`/`POSIXct` bound are arbitrary R: string/character literals are skipped
  and `(` `[` `{` are balanced. Any `,` `|` `>` `<` inside a balanced, string-aware
  `rexpr` belongs to that expression and is **never** seen by the annotation
  tokenizer; only the top-level structural `,` `|` `>` count.
- **`>`** closes the innermost open generic (depth-aware); `vector<...>` and
  `R6<...>` each close one level.
- **`..` is a single maximal-munch token**, valid only in the `length` position;
  a single `.` is a decimal point, valid only inside a numeric bound. An `int` in
  a `length` never carries a decimal.
- **Bullet anatomy.** The `name` is the single token immediately before the first
  ` (`; the `slot` is the balanced-parenthesis group opened by that `(`;
  everything after its matching `)` (optionally a leading `:`) is `description`.
  Names contain no spaces.
- **`scalar` takes no length** — `scalar<T, n>` is a parse error (length lives
  only in `vector<>`).
- **Reference types are bare** — `function`/`R6<Class>` are never wrapped in
  `scalar<>`/`vector<>` and never carry `in`/`| NA`/length (§2).
- **Nesting** of bullets is detected purely by **indentation depth**; the
  trailing `:` is optional and carries no grammatical force.

## 4. Binding & precedence (why `|` is never ambiguous)

After an `atom`/type, the next `|` is resolved by its operand:

| What follows `\|` | Reads as | Level |
|---|---|---|
| `NA` | elements may be missing | element (part of an atom) |
| `NULL` | the whole argument may be `NULL` | slot |
| a type (`atomic` / `reference` / `composite`) | a union alternative | slot |

`NA`/`NULL` are reserved and can never start a type, so each `|` resolves by
look-ahead. `| NA` is valid **only after an atom** (never after a closed
`scalar<>`/`vector<>`, never on `raw`, reference, or composite), and it binds to
the **immediately preceding atom** — the nearest type to its left, at most one
per atom. Thus:

- `(scalar<numeric> | NA)` is **invalid**; write `(scalar<numeric | NA>)`.
- in `(numeric | character | NA)` the `| NA` binds to `character` only — a
  numeric vector, **or** a character vector whose elements may be `NA`.
- in `(numeric in [0, 1] | NA)` the `in` and `| NA` both attach to `numeric`.

## 5. Base types

| Type | Category | R meaning |
|------|----------|-----------|
| `integer` | ordered atomic | integer vector |
| `numeric` | ordered atomic | double vector |
| `Date` | ordered atomic | `Date` vector |
| `POSIXct` | ordered atomic | date-time vector |
| `character` | discrete atomic | character vector |
| `factor` | discrete atomic | factor |
| `complex` | discrete atomic | complex vector |
| `logical` | discrete atomic | `TRUE`/`FALSE` vector |
| `raw` | discrete atomic | raw vector (no `NA`) |
| `function` | reference | a function/closure (length 1) |
| `R6<Class>` | reference | an R6 instance inheriting `Class` (length 1) |
| `list` | composite | a list (a record when refined by bullets) |
| `data.table` | composite | a `data.table` |
| `data.frame` | composite | a `data.frame` |

A `list`/`data.table`/`data.frame` **with** nested bullets is a fixed named-field
structure (a record / typed columns); **without** bullets it is an unconstrained
list/table.

## 6. Every construct, with examples

```r
# --- bare atomic = vector (any length) ---
(character)                       # character vector, length >= 1
(integer)                         # integer vector
(logical)                         # logical vector
(complex)                         # complex vector
(raw)                             # raw vector
(Date)                            # Date vector

# --- scalar (length 1) ---
(scalar<character>)
(scalar<numeric>)
(scalar<complex>)
(scalar<logical | NA>)            # tri-state flag: TRUE / FALSE / NA

# --- reference types: bare, length-1 by nature ---
(function)                        # a single function/closure
(function?)                       # a function, or NULL
(R6<Engine>)                      # a single R6 instance inheriting Engine
(R6<Engine> | NULL)               # an Engine, or NULL

# --- vector with explicit length ---
(vector<numeric, 10>)             # exactly 10
(vector<numeric, 1..10>)          # 1 to 10 inclusive
(vector<integer, 2..>)            # at least 2

# --- intervals (ordered atomics only) ---
(scalar<numeric in [0, 1]>)       # 0 <= x <= 1
(scalar<numeric in ]0, 1[>)       # 0 <  x <  1
(scalar<numeric in ]0, 1]>)       # 0 <  x <= 1
(scalar<numeric in [-1.5, 2.5]>)  # fractional, signed bounds
(scalar<numeric in ]0, Inf[>)     # x > 0
(scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>)     # x >= 1   (Inf is the unbounded sentinel)
(scalar<numeric in ]-Inf, 0]>)    # x <= 0
(numeric in [0, 1])               # every element in [0, 1]
(scalar<Date in [as.Date("2024-01-01"), as.Date("2024-12-31")]>)   # date range

# --- sets / enums (atomics; emitted verbatim) ---
(scalar<character in c("BUY", "SELL")>)   # inline set, scalar
(character in c("BUY", "SELL"))           # vector, every element in the set
(scalar<character in ORDER_SIDE>)         # set from a constant/object
(integer in c(1L, 2L, 3L))                # discrete integer enum
(factor in c("low", "med", "high"))       # constrains realised values
(numeric in c(0.25, 0.5, 1.0))            # discouraged: floating-point ==
(vector<character in c("BUY", "SELL"), 1..10>)   # set constraint AND length

# --- NA permission (atomics, not raw; default: NA not allowed) ---
(numeric | NA)                    # numeric vector, NAs allowed
(scalar<numeric | NA>)            # one numeric or NA
(vector<numeric | NA, 10>)        # 10 numerics, NAs allowed
(numeric in [0, 1] | NA)          # constrained + NA-allowed
(factor in c("low", "med", "high") | NA)   # missing-category factor

# --- nullable slot (whole argument) ---
(scalar<numeric>?)                # one numeric, or NULL
(scalar<numeric> | NULL)          # identical to the above (use one, not both)
(character?)                      # character vector, or NULL

# --- type unions (slot level) ---
(numeric | character)             # a numeric vector OR a character vector
(scalar<numeric> | scalar<character>)
(R6<Reader> | R6<Writer>)         # either R6 class
(data.table | NULL)               # a data.table or NULL

# --- everything at once ---
(vector<numeric in ]0, 1] | NA, 1..100>?)
# NULL, OR a numeric vector of length 1..100 whose elements lie in (0,1] and may be NA
```

## 7. Demo 1 — every inline form on one function

```r
#' Place a batch of orders.
#'
#' @param symbol (scalar<character>) the `BASE/QUOTE` pair.
#' @param sides (character in c("BUY", "SELL")) one side per order.
#' @param quantities (vector<numeric in ]0, Inf[, 1..500>) positive sizes, up to 500.
#' @param limits (vector<numeric in ]0, Inf[ | NA, 1..500>) limit prices; NA = market.
#' @param leverage (scalar<integer in [1, 125]>?) leverage, or NULL for spot.
#' @param tag (scalar<character> | NULL) optional client tag.
#' @param venue (scalar<character in VENUES>) a known venue id.
#' @param tier (scalar<factor in c("retail", "vip")>) account tier.
#' @param dry_run (scalar<logical | NA>) simulate only; NA = use account default.
#' @param not_before (scalar<POSIXct>?) earliest send time, or NULL.
#' @param on_fill (function?) optional fill callback.
#' @return (data.table) the acknowledgements (see Demo 2 for nested returns).
#' @export
place_batch <- function(symbol, sides, quantities, limits, leverage = NULL,
                        tag = NULL, venue, tier, dry_run, not_before = NULL, on_fill = NULL) {
  assert_args_place_batch(symbol, sides, quantities, limits, leverage,
                          tag, venue, tier, dry_run, not_before, on_fill)
  result <- ...
  return(assert_return_place_batch(result))
}
```

## 8. Demo 2 — the kitchen sink: a deeply nested composite return

```r
#' Run a full report.
#'
#' @param symbols (character) one or more `BASE/QUOTE` pairs.
#' @param top_n (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>) rows to keep per section.
#' @return (list) the report:
#' - **status** (scalar<character in c("ok", "partial", "failed")>): overall outcome.
#' - generated_at (scalar<POSIXct>): when the report was produced.
#' - window (scalar<Date in [as.Date("2000-01-01"), as.Date("2100-01-01")]>): as-of date.
#' - sections (list): one entry per requested view:
#'   - matches (data.table): ranked matches:
#'     - symbol (character): the pair.
#'     - score (numeric in [0, 1]): normalised rank score.
#'     - side (factor in c("BUY", "SELL")): order side.
#'     - flags (character | NA): label(s), NA where none apply.
#'   - rejected (data.table | NULL): rows dropped, or NULL if none:
#'     - symbol (character): the pair.
#'     - reason (scalar<character in c("liquidity", "filter", "error")>): why.
#'   - cursor (scalar<character>?): next-page cursor, or NULL at the end.
#' - audit (data.frame): a flat audit log:
#'   - at (POSIXct): event time.
#'   - level (factor in c("info", "warn", "error")): severity.
#'   - message (character): the message.
#' - diagnostics (list): run diagnostics:
#'   - warnings (character): messages (possibly length 0).
#'   - retries (scalar<integer in [0, Inf[>): retry count.
#'   - timings (list): millisecond timings:
#'     - parse_ms (scalar<numeric in [0, Inf[>): parse time.
#'     - run_ms (scalar<numeric in [0, Inf[>): run time.
#'     - per_source (data.table): a row per source:
#'       - source (character): source id.
#'       - ms (numeric in [0, Inf[): time for that source.
#' @export
report <- function(symbols, top_n) {
  assert_args_report(symbols, top_n)
  result <- ...
  return(assert_return_report(result))
}
```

## 9. Demo 3 — an abstract R6 class enforcing every return kind

```r
#' @title AbstractStore
#' @description A store contract; subclasses implement `.impl_*`. Each public
#'   method validates its inputs and its return from the documented types.
AbstractStore <- R6::R6Class(
  "AbstractStore",
  public = list(
    #' @description Fetch records by key.
    #' @param keys (character) keys to fetch.
    #' @param limit (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>?) optional max rows.
    #' @return (data.table) the records:
    #' - key (character): the key.
    #' - value (numeric | NA): the value, NA if unset.
    #' - updated_at (POSIXct): last write time.
    get = function(keys, limit = NULL) {
      assert_args_AbstractStore__get(keys, limit)
      return(assert_return_AbstractStore__get(private$.impl_get(keys, limit)))
    },

    #' @description Count records.
    #' @return (scalar<integer in [0, Inf[>) the count.
    count = function() {
      return(assert_return_AbstractStore__count(private$.impl_count()))
    },

    #' @description Write one record; returns self for chaining.
    #' @param key (scalar<character>) the key.
    #' @param value (scalar<numeric> | NULL) the value, or NULL to clear it.
    #' @return (R6<AbstractStore>) self.
    put = function(key, value) {
      assert_args_AbstractStore__put(key, value)
      private$.impl_put(key, value)
      return(invisible(assert_return_AbstractStore__put(self)))
    },

    #' @description Fetch one record, or NULL if absent.
    #' @param key (scalar<character>) the key.
    #' @return (list | NULL) the record, or NULL:
    #' - key (scalar<character>): the key.
    #' - value (scalar<numeric | NA>): the value.
    find = function(key) {
      assert_args_AbstractStore__find(key)
      return(assert_return_AbstractStore__find(private$.impl_find(key)))
    }
  ),
  private = list(
    .impl_get = function(keys, limit) stop("not implemented"),
    .impl_count = function() stop("not implemented"),
    .impl_put = function(key, value) stop("not implemented"),
    .impl_find = function(key) stop("not implemented")
  )
)
```

## 10. Demo 4 — corner cases and tricky combinations

```r
# union mixing a constrained vector, NA permission, and NULL
(numeric in [0, 1] | NA | character)?
# read: NULL, OR (a numeric vector in [0,1], NAs allowed) OR (a character vector)

# scalar union, nullable
(scalar<integer in [1, 6]> | scalar<character in c("d6")>)?

# length-pinned, NA-allowed, range-constrained, nullable
(vector<numeric in [-1, 1] | NA, 3>?)

# set from a constant, as a vector
(character in CURRENCIES)

# Date / POSIXct intervals (ordered atomics, R-expression bounds)
(scalar<Date in [as.Date("2020-01-01"), as.Date("2025-12-31")]>)
(scalar<POSIXct in [as.POSIXct("2024-01-01 00:00:00", tz = "UTC"), as.POSIXct("2024-12-31 23:59:59", tz = "UTC")]>)

# reference types, nullable / unioned
(function?)                       # a function, or NULL
(R6<Engine> | NULL)               # an Engine, or NULL
(R6<Reader> | R6<Writer>)         # either class

# nested list whose only field is a nullable nested table
(list)
# - page (scalar<integer in [1, Inf[>): page number.
# - rows (data.table | NULL): the page, or NULL when empty:
#   - id (character): identifier.
#   - amount (numeric in ]0, Inf[ | NA): positive amount, NA if pending.

# --- intentionally INVALID (the grammar rejects these) ---
# (scalar<numeric, 1>)            # scalar takes no length
# (scalar<numeric> | NA)          # | NA must be inside <>: use (scalar<numeric | NA>)
# (numeric | NULL?)               # pick one nullability marker, not both
# (complex in [0, 1])             # interval on a non-ordered type
# (character in [0, 1])           # interval on a discrete type (use a set)
# (integer in [0.5, 2.5])         # fractional bounds on integer (use int bounds)
# (logical in c(TRUE, FALSE))     # degenerate set on logical
# (raw | NA)                      # raw has no NA representation
# (scalar<function>)              # function is bare: write (function)
# (vector<function, 3>)           # function is a length-1 reference (scope choice)
# (data.table | NA)               # | NA is element-level; not valid on a composite
# (data.table | data.frame): ...  # bullets need a single composite alternative
```

## 11. Non-goals (deliberately inexpressible)

These are out of scope for the current grammar — express them in prose or a
hand-written check. They are listed so the "every combination" claim is honest;
forbidding `vector<function>` is a scope choice, **not** a claim that a list of
functions is un-R-like.

- **Element-typed / homogeneous collections** — `list<scalar<numeric>>`,
  "a list of callbacks". A `list` with bullets is a named record, not a
  homogeneous collection.
- **Composite cardinality** — "a `data.table` with 1..N rows", "a list of exactly
  3 elements". Length applies only to atomic `vector<>`.
- **A vector of reference types** — `vector<function>` / `vector<R6<...>>`.

## 12. Self-consistency checklist

- [ ] Every base type sits in exactly one category (section 2); modifiers used on it are allowed by that category and its footnotes.
- [ ] `in [interval]` appears only on `integer`/`numeric`/`Date`/`POSIXct`; never on discrete/reference/composite.
- [ ] `in c(set)` appears only on atomics; never on `logical` (degenerate); discouraged on `numeric`/`Date`/`POSIXct` (`==`); on `factor`/`character` it constrains realised values, not declared levels.
- [ ] interval/set bound types match the atom (numeric/`Inf` for integer & numeric; a Date/POSIXct expression for those).
- [ ] `| NA` appears only after an atom (bare or inside `<>`), never on `raw`, reference, or composite; it binds to the nearest atom on its left; never after a closed `scalar<>`/`vector<>`.
- [ ] `?` and `| NULL` mean the same thing and never appear together.
- [ ] `|` is followed only by `NA` (element), `NULL` (slot), or a type (union).
- [ ] intervals use `[ ]` / `] [` only (never `( )`); their inner comma and brackets are invisible to the generic-depth tracker.
- [ ] sets use `c(...)` or a name; never `{ }`; `enum` is not a construct (the word appears only as descriptive prose).
- [ ] a length (`,` n / a..b / a..) appears only inside `vector<>`; `scalar<T, n>` is rejected; `..` is one token, valid only in the length position.
- [ ] `function`/`R6<Class>` appear only as bare reference types — never `scalar<>`/`vector<>`, never with `in`/`| NA`/length.
- [ ] `list`/`data.table`/`data.frame` carry no `in`/`| NA`/length/`vector<>`; they are refined only by nested bullets; bullets = a record, no bullets = an unconstrained collection.
- [ ] nested bullets appear only under a slot with exactly one non-NULL alternative that is a composite; every bullet leaf is itself a valid `slot`.
- [ ] generated names are `assert_args_<fn>` / `assert_return_<fn>`, and `assert_args_<Class>__<method>` for R6 (double underscore).
```
